name: Victor Lewis
abstract: Victor Lewis (born May 20, 1950) is an American jazz drummer, composer, and educator.

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Barron
about_quotes.evidence: Then, when we started working a lot, it was Victor Lewis – who I worked with him before, also – it was Victor Lewis and Rufus Reid.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Barron — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: That quintet was born, the one [that] involved Rufus Reid, Victor Lewis on drums and Cedar Walton, of course, on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: So he says, “Well Victor Lewis is on drums... I loved those guys, so if nothing else I’ll have some great cats to work with... But it turned out to be one of the most incredible experiences.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bley Carla
about_quotes.evidence: He said, “Victor Lewis”. I said, “no problem.” And so I called Victor and got him!
about_quotes.doc_title: Bley Carla — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Watson
about_quotes.evidence: But it’s so nice when Harold, the drummer, or Victor Lewis, or all the guys I’ve had over the years, when they turn it out man, it just inspires you and takes you to another level you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Watson interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, January 19, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...along with Mulgrew Miller, Jay Anderson, and Victor Lewis. INTERVIEWEE: I have it confused with another thing, but I guess that’s the date.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
